content.content: The file /Users/sungjh/Projects/star1/bench/.workspaces/03-todo-cli-ponytail-1SB2ms/src/cli.test.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text:
Done. `src/cli.ts` implements the todo CLI:

- **`add <text>`** — joins the args, appends to the list, writes `todos.json` in cwd.
- **`list`** — prints each stored todo verbatim, one per line, in insertion order; prints nothing when empty (a missing/unreadable file reads as `[]`).

The self-check (`src/cli.test.ts`) runs the real CLI in a temp dir and passes: empty list is silent, items list back in order with exact text, and persistence to `todos.json` is verified.

Two small judgment calls I made: `add` joins multiple args with spaces (so an unquoted `add buy milk` still works), and an unknown/missing command exits 1 with a usage hint on stderr rather than silently doing nothing.

result:
Done. `src/cli.ts` implements the todo CLI:

- **`add <text>`** — joins the args, appends to the list, writes `todos.json` in cwd.
- **`list`** — prints each stored todo verbatim, one per line, in insertion order; prints nothing when empty (a missing/unreadable file reads as `[]`).

The self-check (`src/cli.test.ts`) runs the real CLI in a temp dir and passes: empty list is silent, items list back in order with exact text, and persistence to `todos.json` is verified.

Two small judgment calls I made: `add` joins multiple args with spaces (so an unquoted `add buy milk` still works), and an unknown/missing command exits 1 with a usage hint on stderr rather than silently doing nothing.
